comment: Story-driven advertisement voiceover for the WorldCup26 90s spot. Narrative arc: the world stops to watch -> this time YOU play -> your choice -> the thrill of scoring -> the climb -> the prize -> the bigger story (friends + agent) -> join the world. `at` = start time in seconds, aligned to scene cuts.

lines.text: Every four years, the whole world stops to watch.
But this time, you don't just watch — you play.
Your story starts with one choice: any three of the forty-eight nations.
Lock them in before kickoff — and from the first whistle, they score for you, all tournament long.
But here's the magic — every goal is multiplied, by the odds and by the stage.
Back an underdog, and watch it pay. Up to three times the points.
Three teams. One score — climbing with every result.
And as the goals fly in, you rise — past your rivals, toward the top, in real time.
Waiting at the summit? Forty-eight thousand dollars — and the top ten all share it.
And it grows with every new player who joins the chase.
But the real story is bigger.
Bring your friends along, and when they win, five percent lands right in your pocket.
Or rise to agent: stock up on tickets — sell ten, and the next one's free — plus five percent of everything your players win.
The whole world is playing. Pick your three — at WorldCup twenty-six dot world.
